title: Cleveland Browns' New Stadium: Breaking Ground on a Bright Future
fullText: The Cleveland Browns have begun a mass excavation project for a new stadium scheduled to open in 2029.The new Huntington Bank Field in Brook Park will begin an official groundbreaking ceremony on April 30th of this year. The new enclosed stadium will serve as the centerpiece of Northeast Ohio's largest economic development initiative to date.AdvertisementDesigned and built with the fan and community in mind, the stadium will feature a world-class venue alongside a vibrant mixed-use entertainment district led by award-winning development partner and real estate firm Lincoln Property Company, which will spur long-term economic growth and celebrate the region's spirit and identity.The stadium is set to be a revolutionary adventure and to hold more than just Browns games in the 67,500-seat "super theater"- style venue. The plans include holding concerts, NCAA Final Fours, and international soccer. The project itself will provide thousands of jobs over the next four years in Northeast Ohio."Today marks another exciting milestone moment for the new Huntington Bank Field Stadium," Managing and Principal Partners Dee and Jimmy Haslam said. "We are thrilled to continue to invest not only in this stadium project and mixed-use development, but in Northeast Ohio.""This is more than just a stadium, and we believe that by committing over $2 billion in private investment, we will be part of creating a transformative world-class experience for our community and generate significant economic impact through construction, jobs, and future year-round events."AdvertisementThe move will secure the Cleveland Browns' future in Northeast Ohio for generations to come.This article originally appeared on Touchdown Wire: Cleveland Browns' New Stadium: Breaking Ground on a Bright Future
summary: The move will secure the Cleveland Browns' future in Northeast Ohio for generations to come.
